title: Egyptian Cotton in Argon Bubble Chambers
content:
Hypothesis: Egyptian cotton (Gossypium barbadense) in argon gas shows aether drag anomalies.

Rationale:
- Egyptian cotton has highest crystalline order (40% cellulose Iα)
- Argon is monoatomic (no molecular dipole — minimal interference)
- Aether drag may couple to crystallographic alignment

Experiment:
1. Fill chamber with argon (99.999% pure)
2. Suspend cotton fibers under tension
3. Measure aether drag via torsion pendulum
4. Compare with vacuum baseline

Status: Proposed. Need access to argon chamber.
